INNOVISION: WINS ₹44.6 CR NHAI TOLL COLLECTION CONTRACT 🛣️

• 🏆 Received Letter of Award (LoA) from NHAI for toll collection at the Turup Fee Plaza on the Ranchi Bypass, Jharkhand

• 💰 Contract value stands at ₹44.60 crore

• 📅 Contract is for a period of one year, awarded through a competitive e-tender

• 🚧 Scope includes user fee collection and upkeep & maintenance of adjacent toilet blocks, including consumables

• 🌍 Strengthens Innovision's presence in Jharkhand and expands its portfolio of NHAI toll management contracts

• 📈 Management said the award reinforces its execution track record and positions the company for future NHAI opportunities

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens the company's order book, geographic presence and recurring toll management business.)

PNB HOUSING: DECLARES ₹421.81 CR BORROWER ACCOUNT AS FRAUD ⚠️

• 🚨 Declared Happy Home Corporation borrower account as fraud, involving ₹421.81 crore

• 🏦 Credit facilities were sanctioned and disbursed during 2016–2021

• 📉 The account had already turned NPA and was fully written off in FY23

• 💰 Company clarified there is no financial impact on its current financials or operations

• ⚖️ Fraud classification was carried out as per regulatory guidelines, and the company is pursuing appropriate legal action

🟡 Impact: Neutral (Fraud classification is a regulatory disclosure; the exposure has already been fully provided for and written off.)

ORIENT CEMENT: NCLT ORDERS SHAREHOLDERS' MEETING FOR AMBUJA MERGER 🏗️

• ⚖️ NCLT Ahmedabad has directed Orient Cement to convene a meeting of equity shareholders to consider the proposed merger with Ambuja Cements

• 🗓️ Shareholders' meeting will be held on September 28, 2026 at 10:30 AM through Video Conferencing/Other Audio Visual Means (VC/OAVM)

• 🏢 Shareholders will vote on the Scheme of Amalgamation, with or without modifications

• 📑 The merger scheme is subject to the approval of shareholders and other applicable regulatory requirements

🟢 Impact: Positive (Marks another key regulatory milestone in the merger process with Ambuja Cements.)

PDS: SERVER HARDWARE FAILURE LEADS TO DATA LOSS ⚠️💻

• 🖥️ One of the company's data servers suffered a hardware failure, and the stored data could not be recovered despite restoration efforts and engagement of independent data recovery specialists

• 📂 Lost data included the Structured Digital Database maintained under SEBI (PIT) Regulations for the period November 2025 to June 2026

• 🔐 Company clarified that the incident was not due to a cyber security breach

• 🛡️ PDS said it has a robust risk management framework and is further strengthening its IT systems, controls and data management processes

• 💼 Company stated there is no material impact on its business or financials

🟡 Impact: Neutral (Operational disclosure with no reported business or financial impact, though the loss of regulatory database records is a compliance-related event.)

PAYTM: SEEKS FLEXIBILITY TO USE ₹1,686 CR UNUTILISED IPO FUNDS 💰

• 📋 Board approved proposal to revise utilisation of remaining ₹1,686 crore IPO proceeds, subject to shareholder approval

• 🔄 Funds can be used interchangeably between new business initiatives, acquisitions & strategic partnerships and strengthening Paytm's core ecosystem

• 👥 Capital may be deployed for consumer & merchant acquisition, retention, technology and financial services expansion

• 📅 Proposed to extend the utilisation timeline to March 31, 2029

• 📈 Move aims to provide greater capital allocation flexibility while supporting Paytm's profitable growth strategy

🟢 Impact: Positive (Provides flexibility to deploy capital toward higher-return opportunities and strengthen Paytm's core business.)

• 💵 Board approved an additional investment of up to ₹100 crore in wholly owned subsidiary Paytm Money via a rights issue

• 🚀 Funds will support technology investments, regulatory capital requirements, and expansion of the investment & wealth management business

• 📅 Investment is expected to be completed on or before September 30, 2026, subject to necessary approvals

• 🏦 Since Paytm Money is a 100% owned subsidiary, the rights issue will not change Paytm's shareholding

• 📊 Paytm Money reported FY26 turnover of ₹212.95 crore, up from ₹172.93 crore in FY25

🟢 Impact: Positive (Capital infusion strengthens Paytm Money's growth, technology capabilities and wealth management expansion while retaining full ownership.)

🟢 Impact: Positive (Expands business opportunities, enhances project pipeline and strengthens RITES' consultancy presence across domestic and global infrastructure markets.)

• 🛠️ Agreement includes support for establishing IndiGo's engine MRO (Maintenance, Repair & Overhaul) capabilities in India

• 📦 Long-term support to include spare parts, maintenance services and material support for the expanding fleet

• 📈 Strengthens IndiGo's fleet expansion strategy with enhanced dispatch reliability, predictable maintenance costs and operational efficiency

🟢 Impact: Positive (Secures long-term engine supply, boosts maintenance capabilities in India and supports IndiGo's aggressive fleet growth.)

Financial Highlights
- Revenue ₹349.1 crore, +33% YoY.
- Operating Profit ₹38.0 crore, +41% YoY.

- Strong private utility demand.
- International business expanded.

Order Book & Expansion
- Order book ₹811 crore.
- Strong revenue visibility.
- Capacity expansion on track.
- Long-term demand remains healthy.

Balance Sheet
- Receivable days around 69.
- Borrowings reduced to ₹135.8 crore.
- Cash balance ₹47.0 crore.
- Net debt ₹88.8 crore.

Management Outlook
- Demand remains robust.
- Better product mix supporting margins.
- Infrastructure spending remains tailwind.
- International expansion to drive growth.

Key Takeaway
- Dynamic Cables delivered a strong Q1FY27 with healthy revenue growth, margin expansion and improved profitability. A robust ₹811 crore order book, expanding exports and ongoing capacity expansion provide strong visibility for sustained long-term growth.

Reverse-engineering the small-cap smart money

We pulled the latest portfolios of 6 top small-cap funds (JM, TrustMF, Helios, Bank of India, Motilal Oswal, Abakkus) and ran an overlap screen. What the "best-placed" managers actually agree on:

1. Only 1 stock is held by 3+ of them → Acutaas Chemicals (Ami Organics). Navin Fluorine & Cemindia show up in 2 each.

2. Pharma/CDMO is the only sector all 6 are overweight. Then power/electricals capex (BoI at 12.7%!) and auto ancillaries.

3. The real edge isn't the consensus — it's the high-conviction singles: Garware Hi-Tech (JM 5.6%), Va Tech Wabag (Motilal), MTAR (Helios). One manager, one big call, nobody else in.

But consensus ≠ conviction to buy. Small-caps trade ~33× vs ~28× large-cap, and half these funds are <1yr old. This is a starting universe and one of the multiple ways to screen from — not a buy list.

Disc: Educational research only. Not advice

MIDWEST ENERGY: COMMENCES COMMERCIAL PRODUCTION AT 1.2 GWH BESS FACILITY 🔋

• 🏭 Commenced commercial production at its 1.2 GWh Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) manufacturing facility in Bengaluru

• ⚡ Facility features integrated Cell-to-Container manufacturing, producing battery modules, racks, trays and containerised BESS solutions

• 📦 Offers storage solutions from 3 kWh to 6 MWh, catering to commercial, industrial, renewable and utility-scale applications

• 🌍 Products comply with UL 1973, UL 9540 and UL 9540A standards, with focus on domestic and international markets

🟢 Impact: Positive (Commercial production at the new BESS facility strengthens manufacturing capabilities and positions the company to benefit from growing energy storage demand.)
MIDWEST ENERGY: Midwest Energy inaugurates its Giga Watt Scale manufacture facility for manufacture Battery Energy Storage Systems

SG MART Q1: MANAGEMENT REMAINS CONFIDENT ON GROWTH OUTLOOK 📈

• 🚀 Delivered a strong Q1 FY27 performance, driven by diversified product portfolio and sustained infrastructure & industrial demand

• ⚙️ Value-added products, disciplined sourcing and operational efficiencies supported healthy business momentum despite input cost volatility

• 🤝 Expanding presence in high-growth product categories and stronger customer relationships to support future profitability

• 🎯 Management remains confident of sustaining growth through disciplined execution, operational excellence and efficient capital allocation

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strong demand outlook, improving product mix and disciplined execution support long-term growth.)

LGT GLOBAL HOSPITALITY: ADDS 19 NEW ACCOMMODATION UNITS IN CHENNAI 🏨

• 🏢 Expanded Luxe Living serviced accommodation portfolio with 19 new units across two properties in Chennai

• 🛏️ Added 18 serviced apartment units at Thillai Residency (Korattur) and 1 unit at Vinayaga Flats (Choolaimedu)

• 📍 Expansion strengthens presence in key residential and commercial locations, targeting business, corporate and leisure travellers

• 📈 Company continues to expand its managed accommodation inventory and hospitality offerings across high-growth markets

🟢 Impact: Positive (Expansion enhances inventory, strengthens Chennai presence and supports future revenue growth.)

FLUIDOMAT: CANCELS DEWAS SHED PURCHASE AGREEMENT 🏭

• ❌ Cancelled agreement with Gajra Bevel Gears to purchase a 2,000 sq. m. semi-built-up industrial shed in Dewas (M.P.)

• ⏳ Deal terminated due to prolonged delays in obtaining regulatory approvals, making the project commercially unviable

• 💰 Initial advance of ₹21 lakh has been fully refunded by the seller

• 🏗️ Company said expansion plans remain on track, with the project already progressing on two acres of company-owned land

🟡 Impact: Neutral (Agreement cancelled, but expansion continues using owned land and the advance has been refunded.)

Financial Performance
- Revenue ₹24,648 crore.
- Revenue up 15.9% YoY.
- EBITDA ₹5,015 crore.
- EBITDA up 13.7% YoY.
- EBITDA margin 20.35%.

Operational Highlights
- Domestic volumes up 13.1%.
- Sales volume 39.2 MT.

India Cements Integration
- PAT improved to ₹52 crore.

Sustainability Initiatives
- WHRS capacity reached 434 MW.

Capacity Expansion
- Global capacity target 242.5 MTPA.

Strategic Developments
- Entering wires and cables.
- Business launch preparations underway.
- Diversifying building materials portfolio.
- Vivek Agrawal re-appointed as WTD & CMO.
- Leadership continuity maintained.

Key Takeaway
- UltraTech Cement delivered a better-than-expected Q1FY27 with strong volume growth, healthy profitability and successful integration of India Cements. Aggressive capacity expansion, increasing green energy usage and diversification into the wires & cables business reinforce its long-term leadership in the building materials sector.

ULTRATECH - Solid set of earnings 

Domestic sales volumes stood at 39.2 million tonnes, registering 13.1% growth over the corresponding period of the previous year. 

India Cements Q1 FY27 Normalised PAT at Rs. 52 crores compared to Net loss of Rs. 183 crores in Q1 FY25, supported by 18.5% volume growth, demonstrated UltraTech’s ability to rapidly stabilise, integrate and improve acquired assets

CHEMPLAST SANMAR: EDC PLANT ORDERED TO REMAIN SHUT AFTER FIRE ⚠️

• 🚫 Chief Inspector of Factories, Puducherry has issued a prohibition order on further operations of the EDC plant at Karaikal following the July 17 fire incident

• 🏭 Plant can resume operations only after complying with directives and obtaining a revocation order from the authority

• 🛠️ Company is taking corrective actions and submitting a compliance report with an action plan for revocation

• 📊 Financial and operational impact is still being assessed

🔴 Impact: Negative (Temporary shutdown of the EDC plant could affect production until regulatory approval for restart is received.)

SG MART: TO ACQUIRE TANWAR CARGO FOR ₹85 CR 🏭

• 🤝 Approved acquisition of 100% stake in Tanwar Cargo Solutions Pvt. Ltd. (TCSPL)

• 💰 Deal value of ₹85 crore through cash consideration, based on a registered valuer's report

• 🏢 Upon completion, TCSPL will become a Wholly-Owned Subsidiary of SG Mart

• 📍 Acquisition secures ~9.956 acres of freehold land in Haryana for future manufacturing, warehousing and logistics infrastructure

• 📅 Transaction is expected to be completed by December 31, 2026

🟢 Impact: Positive (Acquisition strengthens SG Mart's long-term expansion plans by securing strategic land for future manufacturing and logistics infrastructure.)

INSURANCE, PB FINTECH ; IRDAI MAY PROPOSE CUT IN COMMISSION RATES ON CREDIT LIFE POLICIES - ETNOW

Insurance regulator may recommend drastic cuts in commission rates on credit protection policies

IRDAI views commission rates earned by lenders marketing credit life insurance as steep

Alert: Credit life insurance is a policy linked to a loan, and repays outstanding debt if borrower passes away

Alert: Lenders market credit protection on loans to cover possible defaults on borrower's death

IRDAI would likely unveil consultation paper on commission distribution rules by July end

New policy may become effective from 2027

Lower commission on credit protection policies may check the pace of mis-selling insurance

Effort-based remuneration and product-wise commission may replace uniform commission structures

Baazar Style has disclosed two civil proceedings against Cupid ltd / related parties

This is interesting because Cupid had recently come in as a strategic investor in Baazar Style through preferential warrants

Cupid subscribed to 1.01 Cr equity warrants at 328.25 per warrant, aggregating to 331.53 Cr. Baazar Style had received 25% upfront amount of 82.88 Cr from Cupid 

And now within a few months, Baazar Style has filed two civil suits against Cupid

The company has claimed a decree of 28.18 Cr along with other consequential reliefs

So basically, Cupid first invests via preferential allotment, and now Baazar Style is taking legal action against Cupid / related parties

What the heck is happening here?

When a strategic investor enters through a large preferential issue & soon after becomes part of litigation with the company, it raises a lot of questions

They also stopped doing concalls which is a big red flag

Vintage Coffee & Beverages | Premiumization Story

FY26 Financial Performance
- Revenue ₹553.1 crore (+79.3% YoY).
- EBITDA ₹99.6 crore (+88.1% YoY).
- PAT ₹72.2 crore (+79.8% YoY).
- EBITDA margin expanded to 18%.

Business Strengths
- Cost-plus pricing protects margins.
- Global export-focused business.
- Debt-free expansion till date.
- Capacity funded through internal accruals.

Premiumization Strategy
- Shifting from bulk to consumer packs.
- Target mix 60% consumer / 40% bulk.
- Focus on higher-value FMCG customers.
- Improves pricing power and customer stickiness.

Freeze-Dried Coffee (FDC) Opportunity
- ₹550 crore FDC project underway.
- Capacity: 5,500 MT.
- Commercial launch targeted Q2 FY28.
- Higher realizations than spray-dried coffee.

Margin Outlook
- FY27 EBITDA margin target ~19%.
- FY28 target 20–21%.
- FY29 target 22–24%.
- Driven by richer product mix.

Capacity Expansion
- Existing capacity expanded.
- 6,500 MT → 11,000 MT.
- Built without dilution.
- Supports future growth.

Key Risks
- Top clients contribute ~50% revenue.
- ₹400 crore debt for FDC.
- Execution risk on FDC project.
- Delay may defer margin expansion.

Investment Thesis
- Structural shift toward value-added products.
- Premium FMCG export positioning.
- Strong earnings growth and execution.
- Potential long-term valuation re-rating.

Key Takeaway
- Vintage Coffee is transforming from a bulk coffee exporter into a value-added global FMCG ingredient supplier. The ₹550 crore freeze-dried coffee project, premium product mix, and expanding margins could drive a structural re-rating, provided execution remains strong and customer concentration and leverage are managed effectively.

Govt Eases Battery PLI Norms to Attract More Storage Players — Final 10 GWh Tender Under ₹18,100 Crore ACC Scheme Floated

The New Tender — Key Details
Government floated new 10 GWh tender for grid-scale energy storage — final part of larger 50 GWh initiative

Under ₹18,100 crore Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cells (ACC)

Floated on 15 July by Ministry of Heavy Industries (MHI)

Tender will close on 13 October; bids to be opened on 14 October

Pre-bid conference to be held on 29 July

Key Changes from 2021 Version — Eased Norms
Easier entry requirements

Shift from energy density to battery efficiency as key metric

Lower subsidies per kilowatt-hour (kWh) — reflecting falling costs of cell manufacturing globally

Minimum and maximum bid capacity thresholds revised to 1 GWh and 4 GWh respectively — to encourage wider participation and attract more bidders for remaining 10 GWh capacity

Revised limits retain same 1:4 minimum-to-maximum bid capacity ratio as earlier 5–20 GWh bidding framework — ensuring consistency with original scheme design

History of the Scheme — Previous Awards
In 2021 (first bidding round): government awarded 50 GWh capacity earmarked for grid-scale storage

MHI called for bids on 15 July — almost two years after the last award under the scheme

Four bidders in previous round:
Ola Electric — 20 GWh
Reliance Industries Ltd — 5 GWh
Rajesh Exports — 5 GWh
Hyundai Global Motors — 20 GWh

After clarifications from Hyundai Motor that 'Hyundai Global Motors' was not associated with the Korean manufacturer — government rescinded the award

Of the 20 GWh capacity rescinded — 10 GWh was awarded to Reliance New Energy (subsidiary of Reliance Industries) in September 2024

Last remaining 10 GWh is what is now being tendered

Per-kWh Subsidy — Reduced
MNRE confirmed per-kWh subsidy has been reduced in this tender

Reflecting falling costs of cell manufacturing globally

Core Theme 
The government's revised ACC PLI tender for the final 10 GWh reflects hard-won lessons from the 2021 round — by lowering entry barriers, shifting to efficiency metrics, reducing per-kWh subsidies in line with global cost trends and revising bid capacity thresholds to attract more participants, the MHI is prioritising broader competition and successful execution over headline capacity numbers, even as India's grid-scale storage ambitions remain critical to managing its fast-growing renewable energy penetration.

ACCIDENT OF THE DAY : WAAREE, PREMIER ENERGIES 

*ALMM-II extension, -ve for Solar mfg, +ve for developers* 

 *What’s new?* 
- MNRE, on Saturday, announced limited extension to the implementation of ALMM List-II (Approved List of Models & Manufacturers for solar cells) for Net Metering and Open Access (OA) solar projects to Dec-26 (earlier: May-26). 
- The decision followed by industry concerns that domestic cell manufacturing capacity remains insufficient to meet near-term demand, potentially delaying projects and increasing costs.
- MNRE stated that the move is intended to facilitate a smoother transition towards domestic cell sourcing while protecting investments already made by developers and module manufacturers.
- Interestingly, the order specifically notes that the extension will also help module manufacturers monetize existing inventories and provide time for the domestic solar cell industry to scale up capacity before the full implementation of ALMM List-II.

 *Our view* 
 
1) Impact on Solar Module Manufacturers (Waaree Energies & Premier Energies)
Near-term impact: Negative
- This extension reduces the value creation opportunity for the manufacturers such as Waaree Energies and Premier Energies with stronger backward intergration
- As the industry manufacturing capacity expands and demand supply mismatch reduces over the next six months, the pricing power for early movers of cell capacity additon would erode for the DCR based modules
- On the non DCR module, there can be an increase in demand in Q2 and Q3, however, given the lower realization and margin compared to DCR modules, this would still be net negative on the FY27 financials of these companies.

2) *Impact on CleanMax* 
 *Near term impact: Positive* 
- For RE open access and rooftop developers like CleanMax, this could have a significant boost in the segment as there can be an increased demand from consumer to benefit from the capex cost savings
- For C&I developer, execution certainty is often more valuable than marginal equipment localization benefits, making this policy amendment an incremental positive for growth and project returns over the next six months.

 *3) Industry impact* 
- In our view, the strong solar capacity addition in FY26 was partly driven by capacity additions ramp up to benefit prior to the change in regulations.
- We earlier, therefore had anticipated the spurt of FY26 (44.6GW addition) to subside marginally in FY27 and estimated a 35GW addition for the year.
- However, the announced extension, we see upside risk to our estimate. BROKERAGE NOTE

RELIANCE INDUSTRIES: DANGOTE REFINERY OUTPUT FALLS TO 3-MONTH LOW ⛽

• 🛢️ Dangote Refinery's operations have dropped to a 3-month low due to ongoing maintenance

• 📉 Crude processing has declined to around 350,000–400,000 barrels/day since 10 July, significantly below normal levels

• 🚢 Lower throughput has reduced exports of gasoline, jet fuel and gasoil at a time when global fuel markets remain tight

• 📈 Tighter global product supply could support higher refining margins (GRMs) for other refiners

🟢 Impact: Positive for Reliance Industries – Higher global refining margins could benefit Reliance's large refining business if supply tightness persists.

WAAREE ENERGIES, PREMIER; ALMM LIST-II DEADLINE EXTENDED; MIXED IMPACT ON SOLAR SECTOR 🔴🔴

• 🗓️ MNRE has extended ALMM List-II (solar cells) implementation for Net Metering & Open Access projects to December 2026 (from May 2026)

• 🏗️ Extension aims to ensure smoother project execution as domestic solar cell capacity is still ramping up and to avoid project delays

• 🔴 Negative for integrated cell manufacturers like Waaree Energies and Premier Energies as near-term pricing power and margin benefits from DCR modules may reduce

• 🟢 Positive for rooftop & Open Access developers such as CleanMax, with lower execution risk, improved project economics and stronger demand over the next six months

• 📦 Module makers also get additional time to monetize existing inventory while domestic cell manufacturing scales up

• ⚡ Analysts see the extension increasing the likelihood of higher-than-expected solar capacity additions in FY27

Impact:
🔴 Negative: Waaree Energies, Premier Energies (near term)
🟢 Positive: Solar project developers, EPC players and rooftop/Open Access segment.

**HDFC Bank | Q1FY27 Jefferies Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- Profit **₹19,100 crore**.
- Up **5% YoY**.
- Results in line.
- Lower costs supported earnings.

**Business Performance**
- Loan growth **16% YoY**.
- Corporate lending increased.
- NIM down **12 bps QoQ**.
- NII growth **7%**.

**Profitability**
- Opex growth moderated.
- Credit costs declined.
- Low slippages supported profits.

**Brokerage View**
- **Jefferies:** Buy, TP **₹1,050**.
- PBT CAGR seen at **15%**.
- Positive long-term outlook.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies remains constructive on HDFC Bank, with strong loan growth, disciplined costs and lower credit costs supporting earnings despite continued margin compression from higher corporate lending.**

SG FINSERVE: PARTNERS WITH BHARATPE MONEY & SUCCESSHIP TO LAUNCH DIGITAL MSME LENDING 🤝💳

• 🚀 SG Finserve has launched a digital lending solution in partnership with BharatPe Money as the Lending Service Provider (LSP) and Succesship Technologies as the technology partner to expand digital credit access for merchants. 

• 📱 The platform enables fully digital, paperless merchant loans with faster approvals, minimal documentation and a seamless borrowing experience, while complying with the RBI's Digital Lending Guidelines. 

• 🏪 The collaboration combines SG Finserve's lending expertise, BharatPe's merchant network, and Succesship's end-to-end digital lending technology to improve formal credit access for MSMEs and merchants across India. 

• 📈 Management said the initiative is aimed at driving financial inclusion by delivering innovative digital financing solutions for micro and small businesses. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The strategic partnership strengthens SG Finserve's digital lending capabilities, expands its MSME reach and supports future loan growth through a technology-led distribution model.)

ELITECON INTERNATIONAL: OUTLINES BUSINESS RECOVERY ROADMAP AS BANKING OPERATIONS NORMALISE 🏦📈

• 🏦 Elitecon International said SEBI has issued a clarification to the concerned banks, and Kotak Mahindra Bank has removed the lien/restriction on the company's account. The company is following up with other banks for implementation of the clarification. 

• ⚖️ The company clarified that the SEBI interim order remains under challenge, with proceedings still pending. It has filed its preliminary response and will continue cooperating with the regulatory process while exercising its legal rights. 

• 🔄 Management said its immediate priorities are normalising banking operations, restoring vendor and customer relationships, supporting employees, strengthening governance and compliance, and completing FY26 financial reporting. 

• 👥 As operations improve, the company also plans to undertake fresh hiring and evaluate new business initiatives in a measured manner. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The restoration of banking operations is a constructive development for business continuity and operational recovery, although regulatory proceedings remain ongoing.)

SUGAR STOCKS ; GOVT PLANS ETHANOL AS COOKING FUEL TO REDUCE LPG IMPORTS ; ET REPORT 

• 🏛️ Petroleum Ministry is preparing a policy to promote ethanol as a mainstream cooking fuel

• 💰 Policy may include subsidies for consumers and support for building the ethanol supply chain

• 📦 Aim is to reduce India's dependence on imported LPG and improve energy security

• 🛢️ Oil marketing companies are developing ethanol-based cooking stoves and fuel dispensing infrastructure

• 🌾 India's ethanol production surplus is expected to support the rollout of the new cooking fuel ecosystem

🟢 Likely Beneficiaries: Praj Industries, Triveni Engineering, Balrampur Chini Mills, Dalmia Bharat Sugar, EID Parry, Shree Renuka Sugars, Bajaj Hindusthan Sugar, Dhampur Bio Organics.

**HDFC Bank | Q1FY27 Bernstein Update**

**Business Performance**
- Steady quarterly performance.
- Healthy balance-sheet growth.
- Asset quality remained resilient.
- Franchise normalization continued.

**Financial Highlights**
- Margin pressure persisted.
- Loan yields softened.
- Funding costs increased.
- NII growth remained constrained.

**Profitability**
- Operating efficiency supported.
- Stable asset quality cushioned.
- Profitability remained resilient.

**Brokerage View**
- **Bernstein:** Outperform, TP **₹1,150**.
- Positive on franchise quality.
- Margin headwinds persist.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Bernstein remains positive on HDFC Bank, citing healthy balance-sheet growth, resilient asset quality and strong operating efficiency, while noting that margin pressure continues to limit near-term earnings growth.**

Competition between US and Chinese AI models is heating up:

The proportion of tokens used by US firms that run through Chinese AI models on OpenRouter is up to a record ~58%.

OpenRouter is a platform that allows developers and companies to access and compare AI models from multiple providers, making it a useful gauge of real-world AI model adoption.

This percentage has TRIPLED since mid-January, overtaking American peers on the platform for the first time in March.

The token share of Chinese models briefly rose as high as 63% in the first week of July.

By comparison, Chinese models accounted for less than 10% of usage at the start of 2025, while US models accounted for ~80%.

DeepSeek has emerged as the most popular choice among American firms in recent months.

Chinese AI models are rapidly gaining market share.

**ICICI Bank | Q1FY27 Bernstein Update**

**Business Performance**
- Strongest loan growth.
- Deposit momentum improved.
- Industry-leading NIM.
- Profitability remained strong.

**Financial Highlights**
- Growth outpaced peers.
- Margins stayed resilient.
- RoA reached **2.5%**.
- Earnings quality remained high.

**Brokerage View**
- **Bernstein:** Market Perform, TP **₹1,550**.
- Best-performing large private bank.
- Balanced valuation view.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Bernstein views ICICI Bank as the best-performing large private bank this quarter, driven by superior loan growth, strong deposit traction, industry-leading margins and a sector-leading 2.5% RoA, while maintaining a Market Perform rating on valuation.**

FORCE MOTORS: ASSOCIATE VP & SENIOR MANAGEMENT PERSONNEL RESIGNS 👤📋

• 📩 Force Motors has announced the resignation of Mr. Amit Pramod Joshi, Associate Vice President (Corporate Strategy) and Senior Management Personnel (SMP), due to personal reasons 

• 📅 Mr. Joshi has been relieved from his duties with effect from July 17, 2026, as per his resignation letter 

• 📝 The company has enclosed the resignation letter, in which Mr. Joshi thanked the management for its support during his tenure and confirmed his decision was based on personal reasons  

🟡 Impact: Neutral (This is a routine senior management resignation for personal reasons and has no direct impact on Force Motors' business operations.)

NTPC: THDC COMMISSIONS 11 MW FLOATING SOLAR PLANT AT KHURJA STPP ☀️⚡

• ▶️ NTPC's subsidiary THDC India Ltd. has declared the Commercial Operation Date (COD) of its 11 MWac Floating Solar Plant on the Raw Water Reservoir of Khurja Super Thermal Power Project (STPP) with effect from July 17, 2026 

• 🌊 The project is a floating solar power plant, enhancing renewable energy generation through efficient utilisation of the reservoir surface 

• ⚡ Following the commissioning, THDC India's total installed and commercial capacity has increased to 3,918 MW 

• 🏭 With this addition, NTPC Group's total installed capacity has reached 90,965 MW, while its commercial capacity stands at 89,885 MW 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The commissioning strengthens NTPC Group's renewable energy portfolio, increases operational capacity and supports its clean energy expansion strategy.)

TORRENT PHARMACEUTICALS: DISSOLVES PHILIPPINES WHOLLY OWNED SUBSIDIARY 🌏📋

• 🏢 Torrent Pharmaceuticals has voluntarily dissolved Curatio Inc., Philippines, its wholly owned subsidiary, with effect from July 10, 2026. The company received official communication regarding the dissolution on July 17, 2026 

• 📉 The dissolved subsidiary had nil turnover (0%) and a net worth of ₹1.3 crore, contributing only 0.02% of the group's net worth in FY26 

• 📝 The entity was dissolved by shortening its term of existence through an amendment to its Articles of Incorporation, and hence no sale consideration or buyer was involved 

• ✅ The transaction is not a related-party transaction and does not involve any scheme of arrangement 

🟡 Impact: Neutral (The dissolved subsidiary had negligible financial contribution, making the restructuring immaterial to Torrent Pharma's overall operations and financials.)

NAVNEET EDUCATION: TO DIVEST PARTIAL STAKE IN K12 TECHNO SERVICES FOR ₹329.68 CRORE 🎓💰

• 🤝 Subsidiary Navneet Learning LLP has signed an agreement to sell part of its stake in K12 Techno Services Pvt. Ltd.

• 💵 Expected consideration: ₹329.68 crore

• 📅 Agreement executed on July 17, 2026; transaction expected to close in around 4 weeks, subject to customary conditions

• 🏢 Buyers are Kenro Capital Education Affiliates II Pte Ltd and Kenro Capital Fund I

• 🚫 The transaction is not a related-party transaction and the buyers are not part of the promoter/promoter group

AVANTEL: RECEIVES ₹20.81 CRORE ORDER FROM L&T 📡📦

• 🤝 Avantel has received a ₹20.81 crore purchase order (including taxes) from Larsen & Toubro (L&T)

• 📡 The order is for supply/services of SATCOM products

• 🇮🇳 The contract is a domestic order

• 📅 The order is scheduled to be executed by July 2027

• 🚫 The order is not a related-party transaction, and promoters have no interest in the awarding entity

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens Avantel's defence and satellite communication order book with a reputed customer like L&T.)

**ICICI Bank | Q1FY27 Nomura Update**

**Financial Highlights**

- Margins remained resilient.
- Strong earnings quality.
- Outperformed expectations.

**Business Performance**
- Sector-leading loan growth.
- Loan growth re-accelerated.

**ICICI Bank**
- Strongest loan growth.
- NIM expanded.
- Fee income remained strong.
- Best operating performance.

**HDFC Bank**
- NII missed estimates.
- Higher NIM compression.
- Margins remained under pressure.

**Axis Bank**
- NII missed estimates.
- NIM compressed further.
- Corporate mix reduced spreads.

**Kotak Mahindra Bank**
- NIM met estimates.
- Results beat forecasts.
- Overall performance solid.

- **Morgan Stanley prefers ICICI Bank for its superior loan growth and margin expansion, while Kotak delivered a positive surprise. HDFC Bank and Axis Bank faced pressure from higher-than-expected NIM compression.**

Piramal Finance | Business Update

Q1FY27 Highlights
- AUM up 25% YoY to ₹1.07 lakh crore.
- PAT jumps 67% YoY to ₹461 crore.
- Retail disbursements rise 44% YoY.
- Borrowing cost declines to 8.8%.

Growth Strategy
- Targets 25% unsecured loan mix.
- Strong traction in digital lending.
- Loan against property performing well.

Expansion Plans
- 67 gold loan branches operational.
- Targets 200 branches by FY27-end.
- Rural expansion remains key focus.

Capital
- Board approves ₹4,000 crore equity raise.
- Growth capital to support expansion.

Key Takeaway
- Piramal Finance delivered a strong Q1FY27 with robust earnings and AUM growth, while accelerating expansion across unsecured, digital, gold and rural lending, supported by planned growth capital.

**JSW Steel | Q1FY27 Citi Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- EBITDA **₹9,370 crore**.
- Up **32% YoY**.
- Beat estimates by **14%**.
- Higher realizations drove growth.
- India EBITDA **₹15,110/ton**.

**Operational Highlights**
- Domestic volumes up **~1% YoY**.
- Weak retail and longs.
- Q2 volumes expected higher.
- Auto price hikes reflected.

**Cost & Margin Outlook**
- Q2 prices may soften.
- Coking coal costs peaked.
- Lower ore costs expected.
- Better operating leverage ahead.

**Balance Sheet**
- Net Debt/EBITDA **1.5x**.
- Comfortable below **2.5x**.

**Brokerage View**
- **Citi:** Sell, TP **₹1,100**.
- Sees EBITDA peak in Q1.
- Valuation remains expensive.

**Key Takeaway**

Siemens Energy - Built for India's Biggest Power Cycle 🔥

India isn't just adding power generation anymore - it's rebuilding the entire grid.

Over FY26–36, the country is expected to invest ~₹7.9 lakh crore in transmission infrastructure as renewable energy reaches ~900GW by FY36. 

Higher renewable penetration (~65% of installed capacity), data centres, grid modernization and interstate transmission will drive demand for 400–765kV transformers, GIS, STATCOMs and HVDC systems.

👉 This is where Siemens Energy stands out.

65% of its order inflow comes from Power Transmission, where it is a technology leader in 400-765kV transformers, switchgear, STATCOMs, grid solutions and future VSC-HVDC projects. 

Exports already contribute ~29% of revenue, supported by Siemens Energy's global manufacturing and project network.

The second growth engine is Power Generation (~35% of order inflow). Around 55% of India's installed large steam turbine fleet and ~25% of gas turbines use Siemens technology, creating a sticky, recurring services business. 

With a ~38GW renovation & modernization pipeline (148 units) and India's nuclear roadmap expanding from 8GW today to 100GW by 2047, the long-term opportunity is much larger than what is currently reflected.

The company is also preparing for demand before it arrives. Transformer manufacturing capacity is being expanded from 15,000 MVA to 60,000 MVA through brownfield and greenfield projects, alongside switchgear expansion, strengthening both domestic execution and export capabilities.

The numbers back the story. Siemens Energy has an ₹18,440 crore+ order backlog (2.4x FY25 sales).

INDIA’S TEXTILE EXPORTERS GAIN EDGE AS UK, EU TRADE DEALS BOOST COMPETITIVENESS: NIKKEI 

India’s textile and apparel exporters are gaining momentum as trade agreements with the U.K. and progress toward an EU trade deal improve market access.

Lower tariffs and preferential access are expected to help Indian exporters compete more effectively against Bangladesh and Pakistan, which have traditionally enjoyed tariff advantages in key export markets.

The agreements could increase export orders, improve capacity utilization, and support long-term growth for India’s textile sector.

ICICI Bank – Q1FY27 FC: Firing on all cylinders

# ICICI Bank reported a 10% beat on PAT/RoA at 2.5% at INR148bn driven by healthy NII growth, and higher other income, partly offset by higher staff expenses and provisions.

# Credit growth was robust and system beating at 19.6% YoY/ 5% QoQ, mainly driven by corporate, rural and business banking, but retail growth remains relatively slow at 12% yoy. 

# NIM improved 4bps QoQ to 4.36% in Q1 while the peers have reported decline in margins. This could be partly due to better int income on RBI balances and lower cash on book. 

# Asset quality remained largely stable with GNPA ratio declining 2bps QoQ at 1.38%. Gross slippages stood lower at INR55.5bn in Q1FY27 versus our expectations of INR60.4bn.

# The bank holds contingency provision of INR131bn, and additional standard asset provision of INR12.8bn made in Q3 2026 as directed by RBI in respect of the agricultural priority sector portfolio.

ICICI has posted an all around strong performance delivering robust growth, margins and profitability. Broker Note

𝗦𝗧𝗟 | 𝗔 𝗟𝗮𝘂𝗻𝗰𝗵 𝘄𝗶𝘁𝗵 𝗕𝗶𝗴 𝗜𝗺𝗽𝗹𝗶𝗰𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀

Sterilite tech has launched 𝗖𝗢𝗡𝗖𝗔𝗧, a 𝗽𝗿𝗲-𝗰𝗼𝗻𝗻𝗲𝗰𝘁𝗼𝗿𝗶𝘇𝗲𝗱 𝗳𝗶𝗯𝗲𝗿 𝗰𝗼𝗻𝗻𝗲𝗰𝘁𝗶𝘃𝗶𝘁𝘆 𝘀𝗼𝗹𝘂𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻, in the US after successful field trials with one of the 𝗹𝗮𝗿𝗴𝗲𝘀𝘁 𝗨𝗦 𝘁𝗲𝗹𝗲𝗰𝗼𝗺 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗼𝗿𝘀.

Instead of technicians spending hours 𝗷𝗼𝗶𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 (𝘀𝗽𝗹𝗶𝗰𝗶𝗻𝗴) fiber cables on-site, CONCAT comes 𝗳𝗮𝗰𝘁𝗼𝗿𝘆-𝗮𝘀𝘀𝗲𝗺𝗯𝗹𝗲𝗱 and is ready to install, reducing deployment time while delivering 𝘂𝗽 𝘁𝗼 𝟳𝟭% 𝗹𝗮𝗯𝗼𝗿 𝗰𝗼𝘀𝘁 𝘀𝗮𝘃𝗶𝗻𝗴𝘀.

- STL is gradually moving from selling 𝗳𝗶𝗯𝗲𝗿 𝗰𝗮𝗯𝗹𝗲𝘀 to selling 𝘀𝗼𝗹𝘂𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀. 
- If products like CONCAT see wider adoption, the company can potentially 𝗲𝗮𝗿𝗻 𝗺𝗼𝗿𝗲 𝗽𝗲𝗿 𝗳𝗶𝗯𝗲𝗿 𝗱𝗲𝗽𝗹𝗼𝘆𝗲𝗱, supporting a better 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗱𝘂𝗰𝘁 𝗺𝗶𝘅 and 𝗺𝗮𝗿𝗴𝗶𝗻𝘀 over time.

- EBITDA loss narrowed sharply.
- TERAI, Snoski and Brothers drove growth.
- NSV trends stabilized sequentially.

Regular & Others
- Revenue ₹256.4 crore.
- Sales volume up 13% YoY.
- EBITDA ₹44 crore.
- Uttar Pradesh growth accelerated.
- Rajasthan demand remained stable.

Manufacturing Business
- Revenue ₹472 crore.
- EBITDA ₹36.8 crore.
- EBITDA up 66% YoY.
- Capacity utilisation reached 89%.
- Ethanol demand remained strong.
- EBITDA per litre improved to ₹6.5.

Operational Highlights
- Higher manufacturing throughput.
- ENA exports supported growth.
- Better product mix.
- Operating efficiencies improved.
- Manufacturing profitability strengthened.

Key Takeaway
- Globus Spirits delivered a strong Q1FY27 with healthy revenue growth, significant margin expansion and robust profitability. Strong performance across premium brands, regular liquor, ethanol manufacturing and improved capacity utilisation positions the company well for sustained earnings growth.

Tata Technologies | Q1FY27 Results

Financial Performance
- Revenue ₹1,664.6 crore.
- Revenue up 33.8% YoY.
- Organic growth 26.9% YoY.
- EBITDA ₹267.4 crore.
- EBITDA margin 16.1%.
- EBIT ₹238.6 crore.
- Net income ₹180.8 crore.
- Net income up 6.1% YoY.

Business Highlights
- Strong execution-driven growth.
- Services revenue up 34.6% YoY.
- Healthy demand environment.
- Large-deal pipeline remains robust.
- Deal conversions improving.
- Customer visibility strengthening.

Operational Highlights
- Workforce at 12,579.
- Attrition moderated to 16.0%.
- AI investments continuing.
- Operational efficiency improving.
- Portfolio diversification progressing.

Management Outlook
- Targets strong double-digit organic growth.
- Engineering demand remains healthy.
- Digital transformation driving opportunities.
- Mobility investments supporting growth.
- Focus on disciplined execution.
- Margins expected to remain resilient.
- Continued investment in future capabilities.

Key Takeaway
- Tata Technologies delivered another strong quarter with broad-based revenue growth, stable margins and improving deal momentum. Management remains confident of sustaining strong double-digit organic growth in FY27, supported by engineering, AI, digital transformation and next-generation mobility investments while maintaining disciplined execution and resilient profitability.

text: SBI Securities Pick of the Day- Himadri Speciality Chemical Ltd., with | TP 800 |

**Kusumgar | Business & Valuation View**

**Business Profile**
- Specialized technical fabrics manufacturer.
- Around **50%** revenue from aerospace & defence.
- Operates in a niche segment.
- No listed domestic peer.

**Brokerage View**
- Emkay has initiated coverage.
- Target price: **₹800**.
- Valuation benchmarked to aerospace & defence peers.

**Valuation Perspective**
- Business remains primarily a textile manufacturer.
- Aerospace exposure enhances growth prospects.
- Premium valuation driven by niche positioning.
- Current valuation leaves limited margin of safety.

**Investment Considerations**
- IPO price of **₹419** offered reasonable valuation.
- Current price around **₹580** appears more demanding.
- Small-cap bull market could support premium multiples.
- Lack of listed peers may sustain valuation premium.

**Key Risks**
- Elevated valuation risk.
- Multiple contraction if sentiment weakens.
- Execution of growth plans remains critical.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Kusumgar offers a unique niche play in specialized technical fabrics with significant exposure to the aerospace & defence sector. While its differentiated business may justify a valuation premium, the sharp post-listing rally has reduced the margin of safety, making future returns increasingly dependent on sustained earnings execution rather than further valuation expansion.**

**@Stockupdate9**

**Jyoti CNC

One more report with 60% upside**

**Unimech Aerospace | Business Update**

**Core Business**
- Derives ~90% of revenue from aerospace tooling, supplying critical tools for Airbus, Boeing, Rolls-Royce, Pratt & Whitney and LEAP engine programs.
- 89% of revenue comes from exports.

**Diversification Push**
- Expanding beyond aerospace into nuclear, defence, semiconductors, energy and oil & gas through organic growth and acquisitions.

**Key Developments**
- Order book surged from ₹93 Cr to ₹314 Cr between Mar'25 and May'26.
- Won a ₹72 Cr NPCIL order for critical equipment in India's nuclear sector.
- Formed a 51:49 JV with Kanoo Group in Saudi Arabia to tap oil & gas, utilities and mining opportunities.
- Acquired Hobel Bellows for ₹450 Cr, adding metallic bellows, tubing and precision assemblies to its portfolio.
- Increased stake in Dheya Engineering to 30%, gaining exposure to micro gas turbines and defence applications.

**Operations**
- Operates through 3 manufacturing facilities with 2.73 lakh sq ft of manufacturing space.
- 5,500+ SKUs across 35 customers in 7 countries.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Unimech Aerospace is leveraging its precision engineering expertise to build a multi-sector presence across high-entry-barrier industries, transforming from a pure-play aerospace tooling company into a diversified precision engineering powerhouse with significantly larger addressable opportunities.**

Brokerage note on DIVIS

Merck's Enlicitide scores FDA approval, +ve for Divi's

- Merck's enlicitide has received USFDA approval.  

- This is a high efficacy, oral cholesterol lowering agent (60% decrease from baseline in LDL-C). We expect this drug likely to see high volumes in developed markets.

- Since we think Divi's is likely to emerge as a supplier, this is a major positive for Divi's Lab. 

- As per our channel checks, one of the dedicated units is likely to be for this molecule.

- India to serve regional markets.

**Key Takeaway**
- **UBS remains positive on ABB India, citing accelerating order momentum, strong demand across high-growth sectors and increased parent investment, which should strengthen manufacturing, innovation and long-term growth prospects.**

**BHEL | UBS Maintains Neutral**

**Brokerage View**
- Maintained **Neutral** rating.
- Target price **₹460**.

**Q1FY27 Performance**
- Revenue grew **40% YoY**.
- EBITDA at **₹500 crore**.
- Revenue beat UBS estimates by **20%**.
- EBITDA beat estimates by **51%**.
- EBITDA margin expanded to **6.5%**.
- Margin exceeded **5.2%** estimate.

**Order Momentum**
- New orders **₹26,700 crore**.
- Driven by large power orders.
- Strong order pipeline maintained.

**Power Business**
- Revenue grew **52% YoY**.
- PBIT margin improved to **9.5%**.
- Execution remained robust.

**Industry Business**
- Orders declined **70% YoY**.
- Revenue increased **12% YoY**.
- PBIT margin moderated to **13.6%**.
- Weak business mix impacted margins.

**Export & Margins**
- Won largest-ever **₹2,300 crore** GTG export order.
- Gross margin expanded **200 bps YoY**.
- Gross margin reached **31.2%**.
- Sequential decline due to mix.

**Key Takeaway**
- **UBS views BHEL's Q1FY27 positively, supported by strong execution, better-than-expected profitability, robust power segment performance and healthy order inflows, while maintaining a Neutral rating as sustained execution and margin delivery remain key to future earnings growth.**

**BHEL | Macquarie Raises Target Price**

**Brokerage View**
- Maintained **Neutral** rating.
- Target price raised to **₹315**.

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Revenue increased **40% YoY**.
- Strong execution momentum.
- EBITDA margin improved to **6.5%**.
- PAT reached **₹380 crore**.

**Order Book**
- New orders at **₹26,740 crore**.
- Orders doubled versus Q1FY26.
- Order backlog at **₹2,60,300 crore**.
- Backlog increased **27% YoY**.
- Healthy revenue visibility ahead.

**Brokerage Concerns**
- Sustained execution remains critical.
- Profitability improvement still challenging.
- High receivables continue to be a key risk.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Macquarie acknowledges BHEL's strong Q1FY27 execution, robust order inflows and expanding backlog, but remains Neutral as sustained execution, margin improvement and reduction in receivables are essential for a meaningful re-rating.**

**BHEL | JPMorgan Maintains Underperform**

**Brokerage View**
- Rating maintained **Underperform**.
- Target price **₹220**.

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Improved execution performance.
- Margin expansion continued.
- Better operating performance.

**Brokerage Concerns**
- Stock up **71%** in one year.
- Up **41%** in last three months.
- Significant outperformance versus Nifty.
- Valuations seen as stretched.

**Order Outlook**
- Thermal ordering peak likely over.
- FY26 total orders declined **19% YoY**.
- Power orders declined **27% YoY**.
- FY27 order inflows may fall **12% YoY**.

**Key Takeaway**
- **While BHEL delivered better execution and margin performance in Q1FY27, JPMorgan believes the market has already priced in the thermal power upcycle and sees limited upside amid slowing order inflows, maintaining an Underperform rating with a ₹220 target price.**

text:
**Gravita India | Multi-Metal Recycling Expansion**

**Business Transformation**
- Diversifying beyond lead.
- Building copper platform.
- Expanding value-added portfolio.

**RMIL Acquisition**
- Acquired **~99%** stake in RMIL.
- Acquisition value **₹560 crore**.
- Copper products capacity **31,200 MTPA**.
- Additional capex **₹160 crore**.
- Mundra recycling capacity **29,400 MTPA**.

**Why Copper?**
- EVs need **3x** more copper.
- Demand from renewables.
- Growth in power transmission.
- Data centre demand rising.
- Infrastructure spending supportive.
- Secondary copper CAGR **~14%**.
- Recycled share **55%** by FY30.

**Business Quality**
- Supplies diversified industries.
- Serves defence sector.
- Around **25%** export revenue.
- Utilisation at **40%**.
- Target **65%** by FY28.

**Margin Opportunity**
- Energy use lower **80–85%**.
- Lower production costs.
- Reduced carbon emissions.
- Focus on value-added products.
- Better margins than commodities.

**Growth Outlook**
- Revenue CAGR **38%**.
- EBITDA CAGR **36%**.
- PAT CAGR **27%**.
- Estimates for **FY26–28E**.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Gravita is evolving into India's next multi-metal recycling platform through its strategic entry into copper recycling, positioning itself to benefit from structural demand across EVs, renewables, power infrastructure and data centres while enhancing margins through value-added copper products.**

**Brokerage View**
- BUY recommendation.
- Strong recovery outlook.
- Expansion into new sectors.

**Key Highlights**
- Target price **₹1,530**.

- Precision aerospace manufacturer.
- Expanding into nuclear sector.
- Entering semiconductor industry.
- Strengthening defence presence.
- Hobel Bellows acquisition.
- Kanoo JV in Saudi Arabia.
- FY26 impacted by tariffs.
- FY27–28 recovery expected.
- Revenue CAGR **74%**.
- EBITDA CAGR **83%**.
- PAT CAGR **57%**.
- EBITDA margin around **35%**.

**Key Risks**
- Customer concentration remains high.
- Export concentration around **90%**.
- Rising working capital cycle.
- Acquisition integration risk.
- Valuation at **97x FY26 P/E**.

**Key Takeaway**
- **MOFSL believes Unimech Aerospace is a high-growth recovery story driven by aerospace, defence, semiconductor and nuclear opportunities, supported by strategic acquisitions and strong earnings growth, although premium valuations and execution risks remain key monitorables.**
